name: Szechwan Curried Coconut Chicken
recipeIngredient: 2 large White new potatoes, peeled and cut into 1 1/2 inch chunks
1 large Onion, cut into wide wedges
recipeInstructions:
1. Separate chicken legs from thighs but keep them whole.
2. In a bowl, combine cornstarch, dry sherry, sugar, salt and white pepper. Stir to coat the chicken, then let stand for 15 minutes to marinate.
3. Heat 2 tbsp oil in a wide frying pan over medium-high heat. Add potatoes and onion wedges, stirring occasionally until cooked through (about 5 minutes). Remove vegetables from drippings and set aside.
4. Add remaining oil to the pan. When hot, add chicken pieces. Cook, turning occasionally, until browned on all sides. Push chicken to one side of the pan.
5. Add curry powder, turmeric, and chili powder to the pan juices, stirring for 30 seconds.
6. Return vegetables to the pan. Stir in chicken broth, coconut milk, and salt. Bring mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and cover. Simmer for 30 minutes.
7. Remove lid and continue cooking for an additional 15 minutes or until the meat is no longer pink and sauce has thickened slightly.
8. Serve with rice and pass condiments in separate bowls.
